1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1300 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 7×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
1.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

1.name: Run Intervals — 4×1000m
1.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×1000m at 10K / tempo pace with 90 sec jog recovery between. Cool down 10 min. Focus on consistent splits, not all-out.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 1500 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Long Ride — 4.5 hr
1.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

1.name: Long Run — 14 mi
1.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

2.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
2.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

2.name: Endurance Swim — 1200 yd
2.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery). Main set — 2×250 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

8.name: Easy Run — 2 mi

8.name: Long Ride — 1.0 hr

8.name: Long Run — 4 mi
8.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

9.name: Bike Threshold — 2×20 min
9.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 2×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

9.name: Mixed/Fartlek Swim — 1100 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 3×400 yd building intensity within each 400 — first 100 easy aerobic, second 100 moderate, third 100 race-rhythm, fourth 100 hard but controlled. 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd easy.

9.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
9.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

9.name: VO2max Run — 4×3 min
9.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×3 min at Z5 (hard — controlled sprint, not all-out) with 90 sec float recovery. Cool down 10 min. Builds raw aerobic ceiling.

9.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 1700 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd. 12×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd. If you don't have a 100yd pace baseline yet, swim at an effort where you can hold a short conversation but feel like you're working. Aim for the same effort on every repeat — consistency matters more than hitting a specific number.

9.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
9.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

9.name: Brick — Bike 3.8 hr
9.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

9.name: Brick — Run 6 mi off the bike
9.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

9.name: Long Run — 14 mi
9.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

11.name: Bike Threshold — 3×20 min
11.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 3×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

11.name: Swim Time Trial — 1400 yd
11.description: Warm up 500 yd with progressive build (last 100 at moderate effort). 400 yd MAX effort — sustained hard, hold form. 4 min easy recovery (back-and-forth jogs or float). 200 yd MAX effort — leave nothing. Cool down 300 yd easy. The engine will recompute your 100yd pace target from these two splits.

14.name: Bike VO2max — 3×5 min
14.description: Warm up 15 min. 3×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

14.name: Race-Pace Sustained Swim — 1200 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery). 3×600 yd at race pace (sustainable hard — what you can hold for the race-distance swim). 45 sec rest between. Cool down 300 yd easy.

14.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
14.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

14.name: Marathon Pace Run — 3 mi
14.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 3 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

14.name: Swim Threshold — 1800 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 7×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 4×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

14.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
14.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

14.name: Brick — Bike 4.8 hr
14.description: Race-simulation brick — ride 240 min at Zone 2 to build durability, then close with 45 min at expected race power (Z3, ~0.78-0.82 IF for 70.3 / 0.62-0.68 IF for full IM). Stay aero through the close. Transition quickly into the run.

14.name: Brick — Run 8 mi off the bike
14.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

14.name: Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
14.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

15.name: Bike VO2max — 4×5 min
15.description: Warm up 15 min. 4×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

15.name: Open Water Skills — 2000 yd
15.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Open water if accessible — otherwise pool with sighting every 6 strokes throughout the main set. 4×100 yd at race-start hard effort (settle into race pace by the end of each 100), 30 sec rest. 6×200 yd at race-rhythm sustained effort with sighting every 6 strokes — bilateral breathing alternating sides. Cool down 200 yd easy. If you have access to a group, practice both lead position and drafting (feet/hip-side).

15.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
15.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

15.name: Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
15.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

15.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1750 yd
15.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation); 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 7×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

15.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
15.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

15.name: Brick — Bike 4.8 hr
15.description: Race-simulation brick — ride 243 min at Zone 2 to build durability, then close with 45 min at expected race power (Z3, ~0.78-0.82 IF for 70.3 / 0.62-0.68 IF for full IM). Stay aero through the close. Transition quickly into the run.

15.name: Brick — Run 8 mi off the bike
15.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

15.name: Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
15.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

16.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
16.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

16.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1400 yd
16.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery); 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 5×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
16.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

16.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
16.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

16.name: Recovery Swim — 1200 yd
16.description: Warm up 200 yd easy. 8×100 yd at easy aerobic effort (20 sec rest). Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Easy Run — 2 mi
16.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

16.name: Long Ride — 1.0 hr
16.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

16.name: Long Run — 3.4 mi
16.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

17.name: Bike VO2max — 6×5 min
17.description: Warm up 15 min. 6×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

17.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1600 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 10×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd. Bilateral breathing on at least half the repeats (alternate sides every 3rd or 5th stroke) so race-day chop or sun glare doesn't dictate which side you're stuck on. If you have access to swim with a group, practice both lead (no draft) and feet/hip-side draft positions — drafting can save ~10–15% of your effort on race day.

17.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
17.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

17.name: Marathon Pace Run — 6 mi
17.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 6 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

17.name: Swim Threshold — 1800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 7×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 4×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
17.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

17.name: Brick — Bike 4.0 hr
17.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

17.name: Brick — Run 7 mi off the bike
17.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

17.name: Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
17.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

18.name: Bike VO2max — 6×5 min
18.description: Warm up 15 min. 6×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

18.name: Endurance Swim — 1500 yd
18.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation). Main set — 2×400 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

18.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
18.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

18.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
18.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

18.name: Swim Threshold — 1800 yd
18.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 7×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 4×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

18.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi

18.name: Brick — Run 8 mi off the bike
18.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

18.name: Long Run — 17 mi
18.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

19.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
19.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

19.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1100 yd

Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

21.name: Bike Openers — 30 min
21.description: Short pre-race sharpener. 20 min easy Z2, then 3×30-second fast-pedal bursts. Legs should feel snappy afterwards.

21.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1100 yd
21.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

21.name: Easy Run — 4 mi
21.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

21.name: Race-Week Activation Swim — 800 yd
21.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×50 yd build accelerations (start easy, finish strong; long rest between — pure neuromuscular sharpener, NOT a hard interval). 2×100 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

21.name: Test full race
21.description: Race day. Swim 2.4mi → Bike 112mi → Run 26.2mi. No add-on training; execute pacing and fueling.

1.explanation: Three swims is a bump in load; the plan parked the extra day on Wednesday instead of Tuesday.
1.science_note: Swim fitness rises slowly; one fewer hard week rarely costs race swim, but it can protect bike and run.

primary_option.label: Keep the third swim on Wednesday

secondary_option.label: Park the third swim and stay at two swims
